QF.id: III-C Fraudulent and Unfair Trade Practices_30

question.text: In the FFSL case, the company raised Rs. 15 crores through preferential allotments. Of this, Rs. 6.57 crore was transferred back to allottees and approximately Rs. 7.94 crore was transferred to connected entities. What percentage of the allotment proceeds was effectively not retained by the company?

answer.text: Approximately 75% (Rs. 11.25 crore out of Rs. 15 crore)
Approximately 52.9% (Rs. 7.94 crore out of Rs. 15 crore)
Approximately 96.7% (Rs. 14.51 crore out of Rs. 15 crore)
Approximately 43.8% (Rs. 6.57 crore out of Rs. 15 crore)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rs. 6.57 crore + Rs. 7.94 crore = Rs. 14.51 crore out of Rs. 15 crore = 96.7%. The company did not utilise the money for its stated purposes. **The Trap:** Candidates may look at only one of the two components rather than the total. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The near-complete diversion of allotment proceeds was the key evidence establishing the preferential allotment as a fraudulent scheme.
